description: A knot in an explanation suddenly comes loose: 'nea' is the recognition that follows. It expresses the moment something is newly understood or a connection becomes clear. 'wao' (wow) reacts to surprise itself, while 'nea' says that something has clicked.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper front teeth for voiced dental 'n' and let air pass through the nose into middle-height front 'e'. Release the contact and lower the jaw into open central 'a' for the separate second syllable. Stress 'ne'.
usage_notes: A quiet delivery suits recognition that settles slowly; a quicker, stronger one suits a sudden solution.
